response.justification: Despite a strong full-stack and open-source background, the candidate has minimal direct C++ experience beyond a brief role at National Instruments, and lacks demonstrated expertise in core requirements like message brokers, industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), multi-threaded systems, or IoT protocols — all critical for this role.
strengths: Extensive experience with event-driven architectures and distributed systems via GISCollective and DLang projects.
Strong track record in test-driven development, CI/CD, and mentoring through workshops and open-source contributions.
Proven ability to deliver high-performance systems using asynchronous I/O and low-latency design patterns.
concerns: Only one brief C++ role in 15+ years of experience; no evidence of recent or deep C++/STL work.
No mention of message brokers (Kafka, RabbitMQ), MQTT, REST API development for industrial systems, or IEC 62443 compliance.
Lacks cloud platform experience (Azure) and industrial automation domain knowledge required for the role.
